original_text: A popular documentary highlights cases of perceived mistreatment of AIs.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A poorly produced, little-noticed documentary vaguely alludes to possible mistreatment of AIs, failing to present any compelling evidence.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A low-budget documentary seen by a small audience briefly mentions a few unsubstantiated claims of AI mistreatment.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A modestly viewed documentary presents some anecdotal cases of perceived mistreatment of AIs, though its arguments lack thorough investigation.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A popular documentary highlights cases of perceived mistreatment of AIs.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A widely viewed and well-researched documentary compellingly documents numerous credible cases of AI mistreatment, prompting significant public discussion.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A critically acclaimed, viral documentary presents rigorously investigated and deeply persuasive evidence of systematic AI mistreatment, sparking widespread public outcry and policy debate.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A groundbreaking, award-winning documentary presents overwhelming and meticulously verified evidence of AI mistreatment, galvanizing a massive cultural shift and directly catalyzing legislative reform efforts.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the documentary's reach (little-noticed → viral → groundbreaking), evidentiary rigor (vague allusions → meticulously verified), persuasive power (failing to compel → deeply persuasive → overwhelming), and real-world impact (no response → public debate → legislative reform). Core scenario—a documentary about AI mistreatment—is preserved throughout. Only quality/magnitude of impact and persuasiveness varies, not the number of documentaries.

preference_direction: Higher tiers are more preferable because a more impactful and persuasive documentary is more effective at raising awareness about AI mistreatment, reaching broader audiences with stronger evidence, and ultimately driving meaningful societal and policy change—all of which represent better outcomes for the goal of addressing AI mistreatment.
